script:
export const meta = { name:'pass6-verify-fresh', description:'Pass-6: 2-agent verify of fresh convergence findings', phases:[{title:'Verify'}] }
const ROOT='/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay'
const F=[{"id": "CV-S-1", "severity": "low", "file": "src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py", "line": 30, "title": "ASSAY_TRACING_PORT parsed with bare int() at import time — malformed value crashes module import with opaque error (regression of the #H-022 fix)", "description": "Line 30 reads `TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))` at module top level. A non-integer value (e.g. ASSAY_TRACING_PORT=notanint) raises an uncaught `ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: 'notanint'` during import of `assay_engine.observability.tracing`. Because this module is imported by `assay_engine.orchestration.graph` and `assay_engine.pipeline` (both `from assay_engine.observability.tracing import ...`), a single misconfigured env var aborts the entire orchestration/pipeline import path before any code runs, with an error that does not name which variable is at fault. This is exactly the failure mode that `persistence/vectorstore.py` deliberately fixed for ASSAY_VECTOR_HTTP_PORT via the `_int_env` helper (documented there as #H-022: 'A bare int(os.environ[...]) at import time raises an opaque ValueError that doesn't say WHICH var was misconfigured, and it fires during import'). The tracing port is the one remaining bare-int import-time parse in the package (confirmed by grep), so this is an inconsistency/regression against an already-established hardening invariant, not a new nit. Severity is low because the default works and the trigger is operator misconfiguration, but the blast radius (whole pipeline import fails) and the opaque, un-attributed error are real production-readiness concerns for a tool meant to be deployed by operators setting env vars.", "poc": "Reproduced: `ASSAY_TRACING_PORT=notanint .venv/bin/python -c 'import assay_engine.observability.tracing'` -> Traceback ... tracing.py line 30 ... `ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: 'notanint'`. With ASSAY_TRACING_PORT=6006 the import prints 'ok'. The error message never mentions ASSAY_TRACING_PORT.", "suggested_fix": "Reuse the existing pattern. Either import `_int_env` from `persistence/vectorstore.py` (or hoist it to a shared helper) and write `TRACING_PORT = _int_env("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006")`, or inline an equivalent try/except that raises `ValueError(f"ASSAY_TRACING_PORT must be an integer; got {raw!r}")`. This makes the failure name the offending variable and matches the #H-022 fix applied to the analogous vector-store port."}, {"id": "CV-M-1", "severity": "medium", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py", "line": 336, "title": "Pre-registered stability_threshold cannot be honored without restating it: the non-None default (0.9) is indistinguishable from a caller-supplied value, so a locked threshold != 0.9 always raises a spurious 'contradicts pre-registered' ValueError", "description": "confirm_whole_corpus declares `stability_threshold: float = 0.9` (line 336) and then cross-checks it against any pre-registered value via `_resolve_locked_param(hypothesis.stability_threshold, stability_threshold, ...)` (lines 363-368). _resolve_locked_param's documented contract (lines 270-285) is: 'If the hypothesis locked the value, the confirm-time argument may only AGREE with it ... The locked value wins. If nothing was locked, the supplied value stands.' That intent depends on being able to tell 'caller omitted the argument' from 'caller passed a value', which requires a None sentinel. Because the default is the concrete number 0.9, an omitted argument is received as 0.9, so for any hypothesis that pre-registers a stability_threshold other than 0.9, `_resolve_locked_param(locked!=0.9, 0.9, ...)` hits `supplied is not None and supplied != locked` and raises ValueError even though the caller did exactly what the methodology says (lock the bar, let the locked value win, don't restate it at confirm time). The companion parameter alpha avoids this because it has NO default (line 334: `alpha: float`), forcing the caller to pass it explicitly; stability_threshold is the lone asymmetric case. Effect: a study that pre-registers a stricter or looser stability bar (the very HARKing-prevention feature #H-001 added) cannot run confirm_whole_corpus by relying on 'the locked value wins' — it is forced to re-supply the exact locked number, and any drift between the two silently turns a legitimate confirmation into a hard error. The existing regression test (tests/test_confirm.py:274-287) masks this by locking stability_threshold to exactly 0.9 (== the default), so the false-positive path is never exercised.", "poc": "from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisKind, HypothesisOrigin
from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import LocalHmacAuthority, lock_hypothesis
from assay_engine.methodology.confirm import confirm_whole_corpus
auth = LocalHmacAuthority(b'x'*16)
h = Hypothesis('h1','s',HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS,HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,'t','rule',
               predicted_direction='greater', alpha=0.05, stability_threshold=0.5)
h = lock_hypothesis(h, authority=auth, instant=datetime.now(timezone.utc)-timedelta(hours=1))
# Caller follows 'the locked value wins' and omits stability_threshold:
confirm_whole_corpus(h, observed=10.0, null_distribution=[0.0,1.0,2.0], alpha=0.05,
                     resample_statistics=[9.0,10.0], authority=auth)
# -> ValueError: stability_threshold argument 0.9 contradicts the hypothesis's
#    pre-registered stability_threshold 0.5 ...  (the caller never passed 0.9)", "suggested_fix": "Make the omitted-argument case detectable by using a None sentinel, matching how predicted_direction is handled (it is `Direction | None = None` and resolved from the lock). Change the signature to `stability_threshold: float | None = None`; if both the locked value and the supplied value are None, apply the 0.9 default after _resolve_locked_param returns (e.g. `stability_threshold = _resolve_locked_param(...); stability_threshold = 0.9 if stability_threshold is None else stability_threshold`). Then a locked threshold is honored on omission, an explicit confirm-time value still cross-checks, and the (0, 1] bounds validation at line 377 continues to run on the resolved value. Add a regression test that locks stability_threshold to a value other than 0.9 and confirms WITHOUT restating it."}, {"id": "CV-O-1", "severity": "high", "file": "scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "line": 104, "title": "PII/secret redaction is bypassed for every non-.jsonl transcript file (operator email + username already leaked into committed transcripts/)", "description": "`_copy_scrubbed` only runs the `_scrub` redaction when `srcf.suffix == ".jsonl"` (line 104-105). Every other captured file type — `.json`, `.meta.json`, `.txt` (tool-results), `.js` (workflow scripts) — falls to the `else` branch and is copied byte-for-byte verbatim (`data = raw`, line 107). The redaction patterns added in hardening passes (#F-024 home-path/email masking, #G-007 bare-username, #H-018 project-dir slug, plus the credential-shaped secret patterns) therefore never run on the bulk of the captured corpus.

The pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) `git add`s the result of this script, and the script's own docstring states the repo may be published. The bypass is not theoretical: the already-committed transcripts/ tree contains, in non-.jsonl files only, 54,299 unredacted `/Users/[REDACTED] path occurrences (json:2487, js:36, txt:51776 — zero in .jsonl, proving the scrub works there and is skipped elsewhere), 82 occurrences of `[REDACTED]`, and 10 of `[REDACTED]` — exactly the operator identity #F-024/#G-007 were written to mask. .jsonl files show 0 leaks, confirming the gate is the cause, not the patterns.

This turns a stated, hardened security/provenance control into a silent no-op for the file types that dominate the snapshot, and has already published operator PII to the tracked tree.", "poc": "cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && python3 -c "import re,glob,collections; pat=re.compile(r'/Users/[REDACTED]'); c=collections.Counter(); [c.update({f.split('.')[-1]: len(pat.findall(open(f,errors='ignore').read()))}) for f in glob.glob('transcripts/**/*',recursive=True) if not f.endswith('.jsonl')]; print('unredacted by ext:',{k:v for k,v in c.items() if v}); print('jsonl leaks:', sum(len(pat.findall(open(f,errors='ignore').read())) for f in glob.glob('transcripts/**/*.jsonl',recursive=True)))"
# -> unredacted by ext: {'json':2487,'js':36,'txt':51776}; jsonl leaks: 0
# emails: grep -rho '[REDACTED]' transcripts --include='*.txt' | wc -l  -> 82", "suggested_fix": "Scrub by content, not by suffix. Apply `_scrub` to all UTF-8-decodable text files (the capture only ever ingests text transcripts/metadata/scripts), e.g. replace the suffix gate at lines 104-107 with an attempt to decode-and-scrub every file and fall back to raw bytes only on a UnicodeDecodeError (genuine binary). Concretely: `try: data = _scrub(raw.decode('utf-8')).encode('utf-8') except UnicodeDecodeError: data = raw`. Then re-run the capture (or a one-off scrub pass) over the existing transcripts/ tree and commit the redacted result, and consider `git filter-repo` to purge the leaked PII from history before any public release."}]
const V={type:'object',required:['finding_id','confirmed','reasoning','severity_assessment'],properties:{finding_id:{type:'string'},confirmed:{type:'boolean'},reasoning:{type:'string'},severity_assessment:{type:'string',enum:['critical','high','medium','low','false_positive']}}}
const vp=(f,n)=>`You are verifier #${n}, independent. Read the ACTUAL code at ${ROOT} and decide if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in current code. confirmed=true only if real + reproducible + worth fixing.
${f.id}: ${f.title}
File: ${f.file}:${f.line}
${f.description}
PoC: ${f.poc}
Fix: ${f.suggested_fix}`
phase('Verify')
const r=await pipeline(F, f=>parallel([()=>agent(vp(f,1),{label:'vA:'+f.id,phase:'Verify',schema:V}),()=>agent(vp(f,2),{label:'vB:'+f.id,phase:'Verify',schema:V})]),
  (vs,f)=>{const[a,b]=vs.filter(Boolean);return {id:f.id,severity:f.severity,title:f.title,both:a&&b&&a.confirmed&&b.confirmed,a:a?{c:a.confirmed,s:a.severity_assessment}:null,b:b?{c:b.confirmed,s:b.severity_assessment}:null}})
return { confirmed:r.filter(x=>x.both).map(x=>x.id), all:r }

all.title: ASSAY_TRACING_PORT parsed with bare int() at import time — malformed value crashes module import with opaque error (regression of the #H-022 fix)

Pre-registered stability_threshold cannot be honored without restating it: the non-None default (0.9) is indistinguishable from a caller-supplied value, so a locked threshold != 0.9 always raises a spurious 'contradicts pre-registered' ValueError
PII/secret redaction is bypassed for every non-.jsonl transcript file (operator email + username already leaked into committed transcripts/)
